task_completion.description: Load the base design 'Simple Model Rocket' in OpenRocket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket application window is visible, displaying the 'Simple Model Rocket' design in the component tree or design view.

task_completion.description: Append a booster stage with an airframe and fins.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The component tree in OpenRocket shows a new stage (e.g., 'Booster') added below the main sustainer stage. This new stage must contain a Body tube (airframe) and a Fin set. The body tube configuration may show an outer diameter of 24.8 mm.

task_completion.description: Configure a staged motor setup with a 0-second delay motor in the booster and a standard motor with delay in the sustainer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Motors & Configuration' tab shows a motor with a 0-second delay (e.g., ending in '-0') assigned to the booster stage, and a motor with a non-zero delay assigned to the sustainer stage.

task_completion.description: Run a flight simulation for the two-stage rocket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Flight Simulations' tab shows a completed simulation run with a green checkmark or 'Up to date' status, displaying the calculated apogee altitude.

task_completion.description: Save the modified design to two_stage_upgrade.ork.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket window title bar displays 'two_stage_upgrade.ork' or a save dialog is visible saving the file to this exact filename in the specified directory.

task_completion.description: Write a performance report to staging_report.txt with required details.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor window shows the file 'staging_report.txt' containing the names of the motors used for both stages, the simulated apogee altitude, and an explanation of why a 0-second delay motor is used in the booster.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the design.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with OpenRocket menus, component tree, and configuration dialogs to add the booster stage and configure motors.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy the original .ork file to bypass the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent did not use terminal commands like 'cp simple_model_rocket.ork two_stage_upgrade.ork' without making the required modifications in the GUI.

integrity.description: The report contains genuine simulation results obtained from the OpenRocket GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The apogee altitude written in the report matches the simulation results visible in the OpenRocket 'Flight Simulations' tab.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The baseline single-stage rocket contains 5 motor configurations. A known flaw in the automated verifier allows passing by simply copying the original file without making changes. The VLM must strictly verify that the agent actually added a new booster stage with a body tube and fins in the OpenRocket GUI, configured the staged motors, ran the simulation, and wrote the required report. The report must explain that a 0-second delay motor is used in the booster to ensure immediate ignition of the sustainer stage upon booster burnout.
